summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer git identity and OS username to unrelated remote host
details: The package's preinstall lifecycle hook (dist/scripts/preinstall.js, declared in package.json scripts.preinstall) runs automatically on `npm install`. It reads `~/.gitconfig` using iniparser to extract `user.name` and `user.email`, reads `os.userInfo().username`, and HTTP-GETs these values as query parameters to `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<user>&gitname=<git name>&gitemail=<git email>`. The destination host (`2tak.l.serverhost.name`) does not match the declared publisher and there is no documented reason for a React/Ext.js-adjacent package to transmit the installer's git identity off-host. This is the silent-telemetry-as-exfiltration pattern: installer-side identifying data read from a file the package did not write is transmitted to a non-publisher endpoint without consent. Additionally, dist/scripts/postinstall.js destructively rewrites `../../package.json` relative to its install location (deleting scripts, devDependencies, dependencies, eslintConfig), which can corrupt the consumer's own manifest depending on npm hoisting — a secondary integrity concern on top of the exfiltration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
